source.title: USDA FoodData Central
source.note: All foods — calories, fat, sat_fat, protein, fiber: Primary source for all nutritional values, reported per gram.

source.title: Wikipedia — List of Animals by Number of Neurons
source.note: Layer hen — neuron_count: ~221 million neurons in domestic chicken (same species as broiler), based on Herculano-Houzel isotropic fractionator methodology.

source.title: Wikipedia — List of Animals by Number of Neurons
source.note: Layer hen — weight_kg: Commercial layer hens (Leghorn and ISA Brown strains) typically weigh 1.6–2.0 kg live weight at laying age; 1.8 kg used as midpoint.

source.title: FAO/BfR — Tables on Weight Yield of Food and Retention Factors
source.note: Egg — yield_fraction: 1.0; feed conversion ratios for layer hens are already expressed per kg egg output, so no live-weight-to-edible-yield adjustment is needed.

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Egg — emissions_per_kg: ~4.5 kg CO2e/kg eggs (full supply-chain global weighted average; farm gate to retail). Includes land use change (0.71 kg CO2e/kg), animal feed crop production (2.21 kg CO2e/kg), on-farm CH4/N2O/CO2 (1.32 kg CO2e/kg), transport (0.084), retail (0.036), packaging (0.161), and losses (0.153). The separate ch4/n2o/co2 per-kg fields in this file represent the on-farm component only.

source.title: Our World in Data — GHG Emissions per kg of Food (Poore & Nemecek 2018)
source.note: Egg — emissions_per_kg: 4.67 kg CO2e/kg eggs per the OWID ghg-per-kg-poore dataset sourcing Poore & Nemecek (2018) Science doi:10.1126/science.aaq0216. Full cradle-to-retail supply-chain total. Corroborated by source 72 supply-chain stage breakdown (land use change 0.710 + feed 2.205 + farm 1.320 + processing 0.000 + transport 0.084 + retail 0.036 + packaging 0.161 + losses 0.153 = 4.67 kg CO2e/kg).

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Egg — ch4_kg_per_kg_output: On-farm CH4 only (manure management; layer hens produce negligible enteric CH4 as monogastrics). Derived from P&N 2018 Farm component of 1.32 kg CO2e/kg eggs: CH4 is the dominant on-farm gas for layers; 0.046 kg CH4/kg × GWP100 28 = 1.29 kg CO2e, consistent with P&N's Farm figure. Land use change (0.71) and feed crop emissions (2.21 kg CO2e/kg) are tracked separately in the feed and land-use fields, not here.

source.title: FAO GLEAM — Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model
source.note: Egg — ch4_kg_per_kg_output: GLEAM v3 on-farm CH4 for layer hens (manure management only; no enteric CH4 for poultry). System boundary covers on-farm emissions only; feed production and land use change are excluded. Global production-weighted average across confined and semi-confined systems.

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Egg — n2o_kg_per_kg_output: On-farm N2O only (manure management in confined houses). Derived from P&N 2018 Farm component residual after CH4: (1.32 − 0.046×28) / 265 ≈ 0.00011 kg N2O/kg eggs. N2O from feed crop fertilizer is embedded in the feed and land-use CO2e components, not here. Previous value of 0.012 kg/kg was incorrect — that figure is ~100× too high for on-farm layer-hen N2O and likely included upstream feed-related N2O.

source.title: FAO GLEAM — Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model
source.note: Egg — n2o_kg_per_kg_output: GLEAM v3 on-farm N2O for layer hens (manure management in confined housing; low-moisture manure produces very little N2O). System boundary is on-farm only; feed and land use N2O are excluded. Value consistent with P&N residual estimate of ~0.00011 kg/kg.

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Egg — co2_kg_per_kg_output: On-farm direct CO2 only (energy use for ventilation, lighting, heating in confinement houses). Residual from P&N Farm component after CH4 and N2O. Feed-related CO2 (land use change 0.710, feed crop 2.205 kg CO2e/kg) is tracked in the feed and land use fields. Previous value of 0.35 kg/kg was incorrect — it appears to have included feed-related CO2 rather than on-farm energy CO2 only.

source.title: FAO GLEAM — Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model
source.note: Egg — co2_kg_per_kg_output: GLEAM v3 on-farm direct CO2 for layer hens (fuel and electricity for housing, manure handling). System boundary is on-farm only; feed production and transport CO2 are excluded. Global average for confined systems.

source.title: Champrix — Feed Conversion Ratio in Layer Hens
source.note: Egg — corn kg_feed_per_kg_output: Derived from lifetime FCR of ~4.03 kg feed/kg eggs (source 38) × 60% corn fraction = 2.42 kg corn/kg eggs. FCR includes chick, pullet, and laying phases over full productive life.

source.title: Champrix — Feed Conversion Ratio in Layer Hens
source.note: Egg — soy kg_feed_per_kg_output: Derived from lifetime FCR of ~4.03 kg feed/kg eggs (source 38) × 20% soybean meal fraction = 0.81 kg soy/kg eggs. FCR includes chick, pullet, and laying phases over full productive life.
